scenario: A user profile component fetches and displays user data.
submitter_context: PR: 'Added profile component with edit toggle. Uses useState for edit mode.'
looks_reasonable_because: Fetch on userId change makes sense
review_summary: The config object recreated each render makes useEffect fire infinitely. Plus no cancellation, no error handling.

issues_found.title: Infinite render loop from object in deps
issues_found.explanation: config is recreated each render (new reference). useEffect sees new dep, fetches, setState triggers render, config new again, fetch... infinite loop. Will hit network 100s of times per second.
issues_found.fix: Move config outside or wrap in useMemo, or inline
issues_found.verification: Add console.log in effect, observe single fire per userId change

issues_found.title: No cancellation - race condition
issues_found.explanation: Rapid userId changes: request A starts, B starts, B returns first, A overwrites. User sees wrong user.
issues_found.verification: Rapidly change userId, verify final user matches

issues_found.title: No error handling
issues_found.explanation: Network failure → setUser never called → infinite spinner
issues_found.fix: Try/catch in async function, set error state

issues_that_arent_issues: useState for editing is fine - simple boolean.
broader_pattern: Inline objects/arrays in useEffect deps cause infinite loops. Always wrap in useMemo or move out.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Spotting that config object recreated each render = infinite loop is a classic gotcha. React Query handles all three issues in one swap.

scenario: useInterval hook updates a timer display.
submitter_context: PR: 'Custom useInterval hook for the timer widget.'
review_summary: callback is captured in stale closure. setInterval calls the old callback forever even when component re-renders.

issues_found.title: Stale callback closure
issues_found.explanation: callback isn't in deps. Effect runs once. The setInterval calls the original callback (count=0 closure) forever. New renders create new callbacks but they're never used.
issues_found.fix: Use useRef to store current callback, ref.current() in interval

issues_found.title: Restarts interval on delay change
issues_found.explanation: When delay changes, interval restarts (correct), but if delay changes rapidly, churn.
issues_found.verification: Verify useInterval hook updates a timer display. by reproducing the failing case, applying the fix, and adding a regression assertion.

broader_pattern: Callbacks in custom hooks: store in ref to avoid stale closure. Don Abramov pattern.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Recognizing the stale-closure pattern in custom hooks; knowing the ref pattern from Dan Abramov's 'Making setInterval Declarative' post.

scenario: Search component fetches results on type.
review_summary: clearTimeout doesn't cancel in-flight requests. Stale results overwrite fresh ones.

issues_found.title: In-flight request not canceled
issues_found.explanation: User types 'a' → timer set → 300ms → fetch starts. User types 'ab' → clearTimeout cancels new timer BUT 'a' fetch already in flight. 'ab' timer fires later, 'a' returns later, overwrites 'ab'.
issues_found.root_cause: clearTimeout only cancels pending timers, not active fetches
issues_found.verification: Type rapidly, verify last result matches last query

issues_found.title: No URL encoding
issues_found.explanation: query with & or = breaks URL: `?q=a&b=c` parses as two params.

issues_found.title: Loading flips on every keystroke
issues_found.explanation: Skeleton flashes during debounce timer.
issues_found.fix: Show loading only when query stable

broader_pattern: Cancel in-flight async via AbortController, not just timers.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Knowing AbortController is needed even with debounce; URLSearchParams for encoding; placeholderData for no-flicker.

review_summary: 'as T' lies. Runtime data isn't validated against User. user.email could be undefined, throwing at runtime.

issues_found.title: Unsafe type assertion
issues_found.explanation: r.json() returns any. 'as T' tells TypeScript trust me, but no runtime check. If API returns {email: null} or omits email, user.email.toLowerCase() crashes.
issues_found.verification: Mock response with missing email, verify error caught

issues_found.title: Ignored response.ok
issues_found.explanation: Response with status 500 still calls .json(). If server returns error JSON, T type is wrong.

issues_found.title: Assumes JSON
issues_found.explanation: Server might return HTML error page on 404. JSON.parse throws.

broader_pattern: Type assertions are lies until validated at runtime. Use schema validation at API boundary.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Recognizing that TypeScript types vanish at runtime. API boundaries need runtime validation.

looks_reasonable_because: Stop propagation prevents inner click closing
review_summary: Missing ARIA, no focus management, no escape key, content not announced.

issues_found.title: No dialog role or aria-modal
issues_found.explanation: Screen reader users don't know this is a modal. Background not hidden, focus stays trapped behind.

issues_found.title: Focus stays on trigger
issues_found.explanation: Focus doesn't move into modal. Tab leaves modal. Close doesn't restore focus.
issues_found.verification: Tab cycles within modal; close returns focus

issues_found.title: Escape doesn't close
issues_found.explanation: Convention is Esc closes modal. Missing.

issues_found.title: Background scrolls
issues_found.explanation: User can scroll page behind modal.
issues_found.fix: overflow: hidden on body during open

issues_found.title: In-tree modal can be clipped
issues_found.explanation: Parent overflow:hidden or transform context could clip modal.

broader_pattern: Modals need: portal, focus trap, escape key, body lock, ARIA. Use Radix or react-aria.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Knowing all 5 modal a11y requirements; recognizing custom modals always miss something; pointing to Radix.

review_summary: Value object recreated every render = every consumer re-renders even if user didn't change. Plus no error if used outside provider.

issues_found.title: Value object recreated each render
issues_found.explanation: New object reference each render. All useContext(UserContext) consumers re-render even if user is identical.
issues_found.verification: Add console.log in consumer, verify only logs on user change

issues_found.title: Direct useContext exposes null type
issues_found.explanation: Consumers call useContext(UserContext), get nullable type. Every use must null-check.
issues_found.fix: useUser hook that throws if outside provider

issues_found.title: Any component can mutate user
issues_found.explanation: All consumers can call setUser. No discipline about who updates auth state.

issues_that_arent_issues: User | null state shape is fine.
broader_pattern: Context patterns: memoize value, provide hook wrapper, expose actions not setters.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Recognizing value object causes cascade re-renders; pattern of action creators vs setters; hook wrapper for null safety.

review_summary: Double-submit possible, no content-type header, no loading state, password validated client-side only, no labels for a11y.

issues_found.title: Double-submit not prevented
issues_found.explanation: Slow network + impatient user clicks Submit twice → two requests, two accounts created.

issues_found.title: Missing Content-Type header
issues_found.explanation: Without 'Content-Type: application/json', body is sent as form-encoded. Server expects JSON, parses badly. Returns 400 but client thinks it succeeded if status check missing.

issues_found.title: No error path on server failure
issues_found.explanation: If r.ok is false (validation error from server), no error shown. User sees nothing.

issues_found.title: No labels
issues_found.explanation: Screen readers don't know what fields are.

issues_found.title: Validation client-side only
issues_found.explanation: Server must also validate. JS-disabled bypasses.

issues_that_arent_issues: window.location.href is OK for full-page reload.
broader_pattern: Forms need: pending state, content-type, error handling, labels, server validation.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Catching all five common form anti-patterns in one read; knowing Content-Type matters; RHF + Zod as the fix template.

review_summary: height: auto can't be transitioned. Animation appears jumpy. Plus layout-triggering animation.

issues_found.title: auto can't transition
issues_found.explanation: CSS can't transition height: 0 → auto. It snaps. The transition declaration is ignored.
issues_found.fix: calc-size() (new) or measure height + use explicit value, or use grid-template-rows trick

issues_found.title: Layout-triggering
issues_found.explanation: Height changes trigger layout/paint. Janks at 60fps with many accordions.

issues_found.title: Content clipped during animation
issues_found.explanation: Necessary but causes nested scroll issues.

issues_that_arent_issues: Inline style is fine for dynamic values.
broader_pattern: Animate via transform/opacity. For height-like effects, grid-template-rows 0fr→1fr is the modern trick.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Knowing height:auto doesn't transition; the grid-template-rows trick; aria-expanded for accordion a11y.

scenario: Utility imports from lodash and date-fns.
submitter_context: PR: 'Added helpers for formatting and grouping.'
review_summary: `import _ from 'lodash'` imports the entire library (~70KB). All locale imports ship even unused. Bundle bloat critical.

issues_found.title: Imports entire lodash
issues_found.explanation: `import _` brings 70KB. groupBy is 1KB.
issues_found.fix: import groupBy from 'lodash/groupBy' OR write 3-line groupBy

issues_found.title: All locales loaded for one used
issues_found.explanation: User in DE locale ships JA, KO, ZH, etc. ~50KB unused.
issues_found.fix: Dynamic import based on user locale

issues_found.title: String access works but loses type
issues_found.explanation: groupBy(items, 'category') uses unsafe string. Loses TS type for category.

issues_that_arent_issues: date-fns usage itself is fine - tree-shakable.
broader_pattern: Default imports from utility libraries = bundle bomb. Always check tree-shaking.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Recognizing default lodash import as the #1 bundle bloat antipattern; dynamic locale loading; tree-shakable lodash-es.

review_summary: No reset (stuck on error forever), no telemetry, no recovery, doesn't catch event handler or async errors.

issues_found.title: Stuck on error forever
issues_found.explanation: Once hasError, no way to recover. Navigation doesn't reset. User must reload.
issues_found.fix: Reset on route change or reset button
issues_found.verification: Trigger error, navigate, verify still error

issues_found.title: No telemetry
issues_found.explanation: Errors silent. No Sentry/logging.

issues_found.title: Only catches render errors
issues_found.explanation: Event handler errors, promise rejections, useEffect errors pass through.

issues_found.title: One error kills whole app
issues_found.explanation: Better: granular boundaries per section.

issues_that_arent_issues: Class component is fine for ErrorBoundary (only way pre-19).
broader_pattern: ErrorBoundaries need: reset mechanism, telemetry, granular scope, plus async error handlers.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Knowing ErrorBoundary doesn't catch async; the resetKeys pattern; need for global handlers.

submitter_context: PR: 'Chat with auto-scroll to latest message.'
review_summary: Missing dep array makes effect run on every render. Plus user can't scroll up - immediately scrolls back down.

issues_found.title: Runs every render
issues_found.explanation: No deps → runs after every render. Including renders from parent. Constant scrolling.

issues_found.title: User can't scroll up
issues_found.explanation: User scrolls up to read history → message arrives → forced back down. Frustrating UX.
issues_found.fix: Only scroll if user near bottom
issues_found.verification: Scroll up, send message, verify position

issues_found.title: Smooth scroll spam
issues_found.explanation: Smooth scroll mid-animation = laggy.

broader_pattern: useEffect without deps = anti-pattern except for very specific cases. Always specify deps.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Knowing 'auto-scroll' has the 'stick-to-bottom' UX pattern; pre-measuring with useLayoutEffect.

review_summary: This is a god component. 600 lines = unmaintainable. Need to split into feature components.

issues_found.title: God component
issues_found.explanation: 600 lines doing 8 things. Impossible to test in isolation. Any change risks unrelated breakage.

issues_found.title: State soup
issues_found.explanation: Related state (filter+sortBy+page) should be one object. Modal state should live with modal.
issues_found.fix: Group related state. useReducer for complex.

issues_found.title: Effect proliferation
issues_found.explanation: Many effects mean many fetch waterfalls. Extract to custom hooks.

issues_found.title: Deep conditional rendering
issues_found.explanation: 400 lines of JSX with conditionals = hard to scan.

issues_that_arent_issues: The fact that it works isn't a defense.
broader_pattern: Components have responsibilities. >300 lines or >5 useState usually means 'should be multiple components'.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Recognizing god component anti-pattern; knowing how to decompose by responsibility; custom hooks for data.

submitter_context: PR: 'Show clickable previews of URLs in comments.'
review_summary: javascript: URLs, missing rel=noopener (tabnapping), image src can be anything, no validation.

issues_found.title: javascript: URL bypass
issues_found.explanation: If user provides 'javascript:alert(1)', clicking link executes script.

issues_found.title: No rel=noopener - tabnapping
issues_found.explanation: Linked page can access window.opener and navigate original tab to phishing site.

issues_found.title: Image src can be anything
issues_found.explanation: User can put anywhere - tracking pixels (privacy), large images (DoS), malformed content.
issues_found.fix: Proxy through your server or validate

issues_that_arent_issues: Showing URL text is fine - React escapes.
broader_pattern: User-provided URLs are attack vectors. Validate scheme, add rel, proxy media.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Knowing javascript: scheme exploits; tabnapping via window.opener; tracking pixel privacy concern.

submitter_context: PR: 'Tooltip with auto-generated ID for aria.'
review_summary: Math.random() generates different IDs on server vs client. Hydration mismatch warning + ARIA disconnect.

issues_found.title: Hydration mismatch
issues_found.explanation: Server generates ID 'abc'. Client generates ID 'xyz'. React throws hydration warning. Worse: aria-describedby='abc' but element has id='xyz'. Screen reader broken.

issues_found.title: Tooltip pattern only on hover
issues_found.explanation: sr-only means tooltip never visible. If 'tooltip' is meant for hover, doesn't show.

broader_pattern: SSR requires deterministic rendering. useId() for IDs. No Date.now(), Math.random(), or new Date() during render.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Recognizing Math.random in render as SSR/CSR mismatch; useId as the React 18+ standard.

scenario: Logger that's used in tight loop.
review_summary: Console.log left in production code. With items.length = 10000, 20000 console calls slow runtime + memory leak in DevTools.

issues_found.title: Production console spam
issues_found.explanation: Every console.log retains reference to logged objects. 20K logs in a session = memory pressure. Plus console serialization is slow.

issues_found.title: Imperative push
issues_found.explanation: items.map(expensiveProcess) is more idiomatic.

broader_pattern: Console.log in production = silent perf and memory leak. Strip in build.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Knowing reporter.write retains references; build-time stripping; gated dev-only logger.

submitter_context: PR: 'Filter products by category and search.'
review_summary: memo is defeated because onClick is recreated each parent render. ProductCard memo is useless.

issues_found.title: memo defeated
issues_found.explanation: Each render of ProductList creates new onClick lambdas. ProductCard's memo compares props, sees new onClick, re-renders. All 50 ProductCards re-render on parent re-render despite memo.
issues_found.fix: useCallback OR pass id + extract handler in card

issues_found.title: Two array passes
issues_found.explanation: filter().filter() = 2 passes. Could be one with &&

issues_found.title: Repeated toLowerCase
issues_found.explanation: search.toLowerCase() runs once per item.

broader_pattern: memo without stable props is useless. Either use React Compiler, or pass primitive props.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Recognizing memo defeat by inline lambda; React Compiler awareness; the 'pass primitive, handle internally' pattern.

review_summary: Missing cleanup leaks listener forever. Also breaks SSR (window undefined).

issues_found.title: Listener leak
issues_found.explanation: Component mounts add listener. Unmount = listener still active. Mount/unmount cycle accumulates listeners forever.

issues_found.title: SSR crash
issues_found.explanation: window.innerWidth during render fails in SSR (window undefined).

issues_found.title: Triggers re-render on every pixel
issues_found.explanation: Resize fires constantly during drag. setState every event = many renders.

broader_pattern: Effect cleanups are non-negotiable. Always return cleanup. SSR-safe means check window.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Always-cleanup discipline; SSR-safe initialization; rAF throttle for resize.

review_summary: No rollback on error. Concurrent saves race. No error handling.

issues_found.title: Save failure leaves wrong state
issues_found.explanation: If PUT fails, optimistic stays as if saved. User sees changes that didn't save.

issues_found.title: Race condition
issues_found.explanation: User edits A (save starts). Edits B (save starts). B finishes first. A finishes - response could overwrite B's optimistic.

issues_found.title: Saves any input
issues_found.explanation: No validation before save.

broader_pattern: Optimistic updates need rollback + race handling. Use React Query mutations.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Knowing optimistic + rollback pattern; React Query mutation lifecycle (onMutate/onError/onSettled).

review_summary: any[] for columns and row casts defeat T. T provides no value as written.

issues_found.title: any defeats T
issues_found.explanation: T<T>({data, columns: any[]}) - T constrains data but columns is any. Field accessor 'row[c.field]' loses type.

issues_found.title: any cast in map
issues_found.explanation: data is T[], but row is cast to any. Type lost.

issues_found.title: Assumes id on T
issues_found.explanation: T might not have id. Compiler doesn't check.

broader_pattern: Generics + any = lost type safety. Each any in a generic component undoes the generic.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Recognizing generic-defeating any; T extends {id} constraint; keyof T for field accessor type safety.

review_summary: Trigger is div (not button), no keyboard nav, no roles, no escape to close, no outside click close, no ARIA.

issues_found.title: Div as button
issues_found.explanation: <div onClick> isn't keyboard-accessible. Screen readers don't announce as interactive.
issues_found.verification: Tab to trigger, Enter to open

issues_found.title: No menu ARIA
issues_found.explanation: role='menu', role='menuitem', aria-expanded, aria-haspopup all missing.

issues_found.title: li not focusable
issues_found.explanation: Arrow keys don't navigate items. Items not focusable.

issues_found.title: Click outside doesn't close
issues_found.explanation: User must click trigger again. Standard expects outside-click close.

issues_found.title: Escape doesn't close
issues_found.explanation: Standard expects Esc to close menus.

broader_pattern: Custom interactive widgets need full keyboard nav + ARIA. Use Radix/react-aria instead.
what_makes_this_review_senior: All 5 a11y issues recognized; Radix as canonical fix; knowing the menu ARIA spec.

review_summary: unreadCount is derived state. useEffect-to-derive is anti-pattern. Use useMemo or inline.

issues_found.title: Derived state via effect
issues_found.explanation: unreadCount is a function of notifications. useEffect + setState is one render delayed (intermediate render with stale count). Standard anti-pattern.
issues_found.verification: React DevTools: unreadCount renders before effect runs

issues_found.title: setState callback OK
issues_found.explanation: This is fine.
issues_found.root_cause: Verify Counting unread notifications. by reproducing the failing case, applying the fix, and adding a regression assertion.
issues_found.fix: Verify Counting unread notifications. by reproducing the failing case, applying the fix, and adding a regression assertion.
issues_found.verification: Verify Counting unread notifications. by reproducing the failing case, applying the fix, and adding a regression assertion.

broader_pattern: Derived state: use useMemo or compute inline. Effect-to-derive causes extra renders and bugs.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Recognizing 'state derivable from other state' anti-pattern; useMemo vs inline depending on cost.

review_summary: Hydration mismatch if SSR. Plus user's locale not used. Plus invalid dates produce 'Invalid Date'.

issues_found.title: Hydration mismatch
issues_found.explanation: Server's locale != client's. Server 'Jan 1, 2024' vs client '1.1.2024'. Hydration warning + UI flip.
issues_found.fix: useEffect to compute after mount, OR explicit locale
issues_found.verification: SSR build with different server locale

issues_found.title: No validation
issues_found.explanation: If joinedAt is malformed, Date is Invalid. toLocaleDateString returns 'Invalid Date'.

issues_found.title: No explicit options
issues_found.explanation: Format varies per locale (short vs long). Inconsistent.

issues_that_arent_issues: Storing dates as ISO strings is fine.
broader_pattern: Date rendering with locales is a hydration trap. Either explicit locale or post-mount render.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Knowing dates + SSR = hydration trap; user locale awareness; invalid date guard.

review_summary: onUpdate dependency causes WS reconnect on every parent render. Plus no error/reconnect handling.

issues_found.title: WS reconnects on parent render
issues_found.explanation: onUpdate as inline lambda from parent = new reference each render. Effect re-runs. WS closes and reopens on every render.

issues_found.title: Connection lost = silent
issues_found.explanation: WebSocket connection drops are common. No reconnection logic.

issues_found.title: Malformed JSON crashes
issues_found.explanation: JSON.parse can throw. Uncaught in onmessage.

broader_pattern: Callbacks in deps = effect re-runs on parent re-render. Use ref pattern.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Callback-in-deps is a classic; ref pattern; need for reconnect + backoff.

review_summary: This pattern is repeated across 30 components. Should be extracted to useConfirm hook.

issues_found.title: Repeated boilerplate
issues_found.explanation: Every delete/dangerous action repeats: showModal state, isDeleting state, error state, modal JSX. 30 instances = 30 places to update.

issues_found.title: Stringifying errors loses info
issues_found.explanation: String(error) gives '[object Error]' or 'Error: msg'. Lose stack.

broader_pattern: Repeated patterns are calls for abstraction. Confirm + async action is a common hook.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Recognizing pattern repetition; extracting confirm + async into reusable hook.

review_summary: Token in localStorage = XSS vulnerable. Open redirect via redirect param. CSRF risk on code exchange.

issues_found.title: Token in localStorage = XSS
issues_found.explanation: Any XSS can read localStorage and steal token. httpOnly cookie is safer.

issues_found.title: Open redirect
issues_found.explanation: User-controlled redirect param. Attacker crafts URL with redirect=evil.com. After login, user redirected to evil. Phishing vector.

issues_found.title: CSRF on code exchange
issues_found.explanation: GET requests can be CSRF'd. State param missing.

broader_pattern: Auth flows: tokens in cookies, validate redirects, CSRF protection.
what_makes_this_review_senior: Knowing OAuth has CSRF protection via state; open redirect attack; cookies over localStorage.
